Virga caroliniana, also known as American staff tree or staff tree, is a shrub or small tree native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, offering potential health benefits in treating wounds, fever, and other ailments. Bioactive constituents of Virga caroliniana include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
